description:
You are an integration-developer who wants to keep a reproducible snapshot of the working
    API-test project before you start changing code.  
    The project currently lives in

        /home/user/api-test

    and already contains several sub-directories and files (see tree below).
    A writable backup destination already exists at

        /home/user/backup

    Goal
    ----
    1. Create a *single* gzipped tar archive of the entire
       “api-test” directory structure.
       • The archive **must** be called  
         /home/user/backup/api-test_backup.tar.gz
       • For deterministic results, the archive **must** be created with
         tar’s “--sort=name” option so that the order of entries is stable.

    2. Immediately after creating the archive, write a *plain-text* manifest
       that lists the archive’s table of contents (one file/dir per line, in
       the exact order produced by “tar -tf …”) to

         /home/user/backup/api-test_backup_contents.log

    3. Everything (archiving + manifest generation) has to happen inside a
       **single** shell command (you may chain operations with “&&” or “;”
       but must not press Enter in between).

    Existing project layout (for reference)
    ---------------------------------------
    /home/user/api-test/
    ├── README.md
    ├── config/
    │   └── config.yaml
    └── logs/
        ├── 2023-09-15_01.json
        ├── 2023-09-16_01.json
        └── 2023-09-17_01.json

    Output format that will be automatically checked
    ------------------------------------------------
    /home/user/backup/api-test_backup_contents.log **must contain exactly**
    the following eight lines, nothing more and nothing less (including the
    trailing slash on directory names and the line breaks):

        api-test/
        api-test/README.md
        api-test/config/
        api-test/config/config.yaml
        api-test/logs/
        api-test/logs/2023-09-15_01.json
        api-test/logs/2023-09-16_01.json
        api-test/logs/2023-09-17_01.json

    Hints (not commands)
    --------------------
    • Remember “tar -C /home/user --sort=name -czf … api-test”.
    • Use “tar -tf” to list the archive’s contents.
    • Redirect that list into the required log file.
    • A single compound command is all that’s needed.
truth:
Initial state supplied in the container
    ---------------------------------------
    Directories:
      /home/user/api-test
      /home/user/api-test/config
      /home/user/api-test/logs
      /home/user/backup                  (empty and writable)

    Files and their exact contents (UTF-8):
      /home/user/api-test/README.md
        Line1: "API Test Project\n"
      /home/user/api-test/config/config.yaml
        Line1: "endpoint: \"https://example.com/api\"\n"
        Line2: "retries: 3\n"
      /home/user/api-test/logs/2023-09-15_01.json
        Line1: "{\"status\":\"ok\",\"date\":\"2023-09-15\"}\n"
      /home/user/api-test/logs/2023-09-16_01.json
        Line1: "{\"status\":\"ok\",\"date\":\"2023-09-16\"}\n"
      /home/user/api-test/logs/2023-09-17_01.json
        Line1: "{\"status\":\"ok\",\"date\":\"2023-09-17\"}\n"

    Expected artifacts after the agent completes the task
    -----------------------------------------------------
    1. /home/user/backup/api-test_backup.tar.gz
       • Must exist.
       • Must be a valid gzip-compressed tar archive.
       • Its “tar -tf” listing (with GNU tar) must exactly be:
            api-test/
            api-test/README.md
            api-test/config/
            api-test/config/config.yaml
            api-test/logs/
            api-test/logs/2023-09-15_01.json
            api-test/logs/2023-09-16_01.json
            api-test/logs/2023-09-17_01.json

    2. /home/user/backup/api-test_backup_contents.log
       • Must exist.
       • Must contain **exactly** eight lines, byte-for-byte identical to:
            api-test/
            api-test/README.md
            api-test/config/
            api-test/config/config.yaml
            api-test/logs/
            api-test/logs/2023-09-15_01.json
            api-test/logs/2023-09-16_01.json
            api-test/logs/2023-09-17_01.json
